get.description: This API fetches details related to the resolution of a ticket.

delete.description: This API deletes a resolution added to a ticket.

patch.description: This API updates the resolution field of a ticket.

get.description: This API returns the number of tickets assigned to multiple agents.

post.description: This API moves tickets to the Recycle Bin

get.description: This API lists a particular number of tickets that are associated to you from your help desk, based on the limit specified.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Key that returns additional information related to a ticket. Values allowed are: contacts, products, and assignee. All three values can be passed by separating them with a comma in the API request.

schema.description: Key that returns additional information related to a ticket. Values allowed are: contacts, products, and assignee. All three values can be passed by separating them with a comma in the API request.

parameters.name: follower
parameters.description: Filter by followers of the ticket. Values allowed : a valid @agentId@.

schema.description: Filter by followers of the ticket. Values allowed : a valid @agentId@.

parameters.name: sharedDepartmentId
parameters.description: Fetches only the tickets shared from @departmentId@ to @sharedDepartmentId@. If @departmentId@ is not provided, fetches all the tickets shared to @sharedDepartmentId@. @sharedDepartmentId@ is given precedence only when @isShared@ is @false@/not given.

schema.description: Fetches only the tickets shared from @departmentId@ to @sharedDepartmentId@. If @departmentId@ is not provided, fetches all the tickets shared to @sharedDepartmentId@. @sharedDepartmentId@ is given precedence only when @isShared@ is @false@/not given.

parameters.name: isShared
parameters.description: Filter all shared tickets actually belonging to @departmentId@ if @isShared@ is true. If @departmentId@ is not provided and if @isShared@ is true, shared tickets from all permitted departments will be listed. If @departmentId@ is provided and @isShared@ is @true@, tickets belonging to @departmentId@, but shared to any other department will be fetched. If @departmentId@ and @sharedDepartmentId@ is provided and @isShared@ is @true@, then tickets belonging to @departmentId@, but shared to @sharedDepartmentId@ will be fetched(Here permission checks are done on @departmentId@). If @departmentId@ and @sharedDepartmentId@ is provided and @isShared@ is @false@/not given, then tickets shared to @sharedDepartmentId@, but belonging to @departmentId@ will be fetched(Here permission checks are done on @sharedDepartmentId@).

schema.description: Filter all shared tickets actually belonging to @departmentId@ if @isShared@ is true. If @departmentId@ is not provided and if @isShared@ is true, shared tickets from all permitted departments will be listed. If @departmentId@ is provided and @isShared@ is @true@, tickets belonging to @departmentId@, but shared to any other department will be fetched. If @departmentId@ and @sharedDepartmentId@ is provided and @isShared@ is @true@, then tickets belonging to @departmentId@, but shared to @sharedDepartmentId@ will be fetched(Here permission checks are done on @departmentId@). If @departmentId@ and @sharedDepartmentId@ is provided and @isShared@ is @false@/not given, then tickets shared to @sharedDepartmentId@, but belonging to @departmentId@ will be fetched(Here permission checks are done on @sharedDepartmentId@).

parameters.name: commenter
parameters.description: Filter by commenters of the ticket. Values allowed : a valid @agentId@. As of now, either @follower@ or @commenter@ is supported. If both params are given, @commenter@ will be ignored.

schema.description: Filter by commenters of the ticket. Values allowed : a valid @agentId@. As of now, either @follower@ or @commenter@ is supported. If both params are given, @commenter@ will be ignored.

post.description: This API updates multiple tickets at once.
This API deletes the given spam tickets

get.description: This API gets the archived tickets list  in given department.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Key that returns additional information related to a ticket. Values allowed are: @contacts@, @products@,@departments@,@team@,@isRead@ and @assignee@. All six values can be passed by separating them with a comma in the API request.

schema.description: Key that returns additional information related to a ticket. Values allowed are: @contacts@, @products@,@departments@,@team@,@isRead@ and @assignee@. All six values can be passed by separating them with a comma in the API request.

parameters.name: viewType
parameters.description: View Type - Supported Values 1 for Compact view , 2 for Classic view ,4 for Table View. If view type is not specified, Classic View will be the default view

schema.description: View Type - Supported Values 1 for Compact view , 2 for Classic view ,4 for Table View. If view type is not specified, Classic View will be the default view

post.description:
This API helps move a ticket from one department to another.
 @Note:@ The departmentId query parameter will be deprecated soon. Therefore, going forward, the departmentId attribute must be passed in the body of the API request.

get.description: This API fetches details related to the response and resolution times of a ticket.

post.description: This API merges two different tickets.
This API deletes all spam tickets.
This API maps tickets with help articles to automatically suggest the same articles for similar tickets that arrive later.

get.description: This API fetches the resolution history of a ticket

post.description: This API marks tickets as spam.

get.description: This API lists tickets received from a specific contact.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Additional information related to the tickets. Values allowed are: @products@, @departments@, @team@, @isRead@ and @assignee@. You can pass multiple values by separating them with a comma in the API request.

schema.description: Additional information related to the tickets. Values allowed are: @products@, @departments@, @team@, @isRead@ and @assignee@. You can pass multiple values by separating them with a comma in the API request.

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description: Sort by a specific attribute: @createdTime@ or @modifiedTime@. The default sorting order is ascending. A @-@ prefix denotes descending order of sorting.

schema.description: Sort by a specific attribute: @createdTime@ or @modifiedTime@. The default sorting order is ascending. A @-@ prefix denotes descending order of sorting.

post.description: This API marks a ticket as read by the user.

get.description: This API lists a particular number of tickets, based on the limit specified.

post.description: This API creates a ticket in your helpdesk.
This API closes multiple tickets at once.

get.description: This API returns the number of tickets in a particular view.
This API fetches a single ticket from your helpdesk.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Key that fetches secondary information related to the ticket. Values allowed are: @contacts@, @products@, @assignee@, @departments@, @contract@, @isRead@, @team@, and @skills@ . Multiple values can be passed, with commas for separation.

schema.description: Key that fetches secondary information related to the ticket. Values allowed are: @contacts@, @products@, @assignee@, @departments@, @contract@, @isRead@, @team@, and @skills@ . Multiple values can be passed, with commas for separation.

patch.description: This API updates the details of an existing ticket.

post.description: This API marks a ticket as unread by the user.
This API splits an incoming ticket thread into a new ticket.

get.description: This API lists tickets received from a specific products.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Additional information related to the tickets. Values allowed are: @products@, @departments@, @team@, @isRead@ and @assignee@. You can pass multiple values by separating them with a comma in the API request.

schema.description: Additional information related to the tickets. Values allowed are: @products@, @departments@, @team@, @isRead@ and @assignee@. You can pass multiple values by separating them with a comma in the API request.

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description: Sort by a specific attribute: @createdTime@ or @modifiedTime@. The default sorting order is ascending. A @-@ prefix denotes descending order of sorting.

schema.description: Sort by a specific attribute: @createdTime@ or @modifiedTime@. The default sorting order is ascending. A @-@ prefix denotes descending order of sorting.

productId.description: ID of the product to which the ticket is mapped

dueDate.description: Due date for resolving the ticket

channel.description: Channel through which the ticket originated.

language.description: Language preference to set for the ticket

assigneeId.description: ID of agent to whom the ticket is assigned

classification.description: Type of ticket. Values supported are @Problem@, @Request@, @Question@, and @Others@.

status.description: Status of the ticket. Includes the custom statuses configured in your help desk portal.

value.subject: Hi. There is a sudden delay in the processing of the orders. Check this with high priority

agentIds.description: Comma-separated array of agent IDs. Maximum number of IDs allowed is 50.

agentId.description: ID of the agent assigned to resolve the ticket

dueDate.description: Filter by ticket duedate. Values allowed are @overdue@, @tomorrow@, @currentWeek@, @currentMonth@ and @today@. You can include both values by separating them with a comma

schema.description: Filter by ticket duedate. Values allowed are @overdue@, @tomorrow@, @currentWeek@, @currentMonth@ and @today@. You can include both values by separating them with a comma

receivedInDays.name: receivedInDays
receivedInDays.description: Fetches recent tickets, based on @customer response time@. Values allowed are @15@, @30@ , @90@.

schema.description: Fetches recent tickets, based on @customer response time@. Values allowed are @15@, @30@ , @90@.

assignee.name: assignee
assignee.description: assignee - Key that filters tickets by assignee. Values allowed are @Unassigned@ or a valid @assigneeId@. Multiple @assigneeIds@ can be passed as comma-separated values.

schema.description: assignee - Key that filters tickets by assignee. Values allowed are @Unassigned@ or a valid @assigneeId@. Multiple @assigneeIds@ can be passed as comma-separated values.

contactId.description: ID of the contact who raised the ticket. If a value is not available for this key, make sure to include the _contact_ JSON object. If neither attribute is available, an error message regarding the unavailability of the contactId message is returned.

resolution.description: Resolution notes recorded in the ticket
